status: Illustrative typed price scenarios. Verify the live project, methodology, delivery, registry retirement, fees, minimum purchase size, and project-specific evidence before purchase.

avoided_or_reduced_emissions.description: Funds a project credited with preventing or reducing emissions relative to a counterfactual baseline. It does not remove the repository's emitted CO2 from the atmosphere.
avoided_or_reduced_emissions.storage_medium: not applicable: the claim is avoided or reduced emissions
avoided_or_reduced_emissions.durability: depends on the intervention and continued operation
avoided_or_reduced_emissions.delivery: prefer ex-post issued and retired credits
avoided_or_reduced_emissions.uncertainty_profile: baseline choice, additionality, leakage, rebound, and double-counting risk
avoided_or_reduced_emissions.claim_guidance: Report as financing an emission reduction or avoidance contribution, not as physical removal of an already-emitted tonne.
avoided_or_reduced_emissions.notes: Quality and minimum purchases vary substantially by project.

nature_based_removal.removal_pathway: afforestation, reforestation, ecosystem restoration, or soil carbon
nature_based_removal.description: Removes atmospheric CO2 through biological growth, while storing it in carbon pools that may reverse or saturate.
nature_based_removal.storage_medium: living biomass, dead biomass, or soil organic carbon
nature_based_removal.durability: typically decades to centuries, with material reversal risk
nature_based_removal.delivery: prefer ex-post measured removals with buffers and ongoing monitoring
nature_based_removal.uncertainty_profile: measurement, baseline, additionality, fire, disease, land-use change, leakage, and stewardship
nature_based_removal.claim_guidance: Report as removal with reversible biological storage; retain buffer-pool and monitoring details.

biochar_carbon_removal.description: Biomass removes atmospheric CO2; controlled pyrolysis converts part of that carbon into a comparatively stable solid store.
biochar_carbon_removal.storage_medium: stable pyrogenic carbon in soil or durable materials
biochar_carbon_removal.durability: commonly credited on a centennial basis; methodology and measured stable fraction matter
biochar_carbon_removal.delivery: prefer ex-post production, application tracking, lifecycle accounting, and registry retirement
biochar_carbon_removal.uncertainty_profile: feedstock counterfactual, pyrolysis emissions, stable-carbon fraction, lifecycle deductions, end-use tracking, and decay model
biochar_carbon_removal.claim_guidance: Report as physical removal with durable but not perfectly permanent storage; retain feedstock, production, laboratory, lifecycle, and end-use evidence.

geological_or_mineral_removal.removal_pathway: direct air capture, biomass-derived CO2 storage, mineralization, or similar
geological_or_mineral_removal.description: Removes atmospheric or biogenic CO2 and stores it in geological formations or stable minerals.
geological_or_mineral_removal.storage_medium: geological reservoir or stable carbonate/mineral phase
geological_or_mineral_removal.durability: generally centuries to millennia when storage and monitoring are sound
geological_or_mineral_removal.delivery: distinguish delivered ex-post removal from future offtake or pre-purchase
geological_or_mineral_removal.uncertainty_profile: net lifecycle removal, energy source, capture efficiency, transport, storage monitoring, delivery risk, and scale-up
geological_or_mineral_removal.claim_guidance: Report delivered and future removals separately; do not count an undelivered pre-purchase as completed compensation.
geological_or_mineral_removal.source: Representative durable-removal contract and retail pricing
